inline_comments.message:
its always trick with these long names and when they keep changing.
Not sure if we discussed this before and what exactly we agreed. I would consider 2 options
1) consistent use long name for event ie. DataJobSubscriptionDmiInEventProducers
2) use short names in the context of package names, so in this case
datajob/dmi/EventProducer would suffice

Its the halfway house I never like. I think in this case I would prefer option 2
as per previous comment I think just 'send' might be OK here given the context of the class
as per previous comment I think just 'event' might be OK here given the context of the class

is this really going to be variable?
(or is this create, delete etc?)
I am getting a bit lost in this class so I think I need a walkthrough
I would remove this word, it suggest there is a Synchronous interface and it is not needed to understand wata the method does

IS this methof applicablt for create and delete?!
send(Create)EventToDmis  OR
send(Create)EventToAffectedDmis
no need to add the end-goals to method names, the methow doesn't care who or what uses it and it should be irrelevant to the code

is this needed? Doe steh query not already have filter this?
(Or are you trying to handle the delete case here too?!)

simplify by assigning to existing value in map (else block) or creata new one(if block)
Add new dat outside if-else block
see above: no need for this method if you add data outside if-else block above

should NOT continue with empty string, that will lead to other problems and needless processing.
Throw exception and have calling method catch it and record details
(alternatively use optional instead of exception to control the flow in the calling method)
Doesn't really make a difference so I would NOT do this for better performance

'alternate id matcher always returns a cm handle id'

this will shorten the subsequent calls to fetch the nested objects.

agree its could be an issue but myself and Lee Anjella thought it wa better to keep the name short and that the package structure helps resolving what exactly it is

usually I follow this convention hence I suggested. I have used at other places in our codebase as well.
The parameter which is mutated is kept at the last. Helps to separate b/w input and output params.
